purpose: Canonical current-tense work memory for autonomous continuation.

current.goal: Use the layered doc entrypoint (`docs/prd.md`, `docs/execution-model.md`, `docs/current-state.md`), the Python-first PRD set, and the two-tier RAG surface to keep lower-capacity autonomous implementation moving across consecutive one-commit in-bounds tasks without relying on removed TypeScript assets or ad hoc search expansion.
current.milestone: Low-attention autonomous continuation remains unambiguous under the Python-first PRD contract
current.invariant: OpenClaw remains the only browser-facing actor, ez-ax remains orchestration-centric, the released ceiling remains pageReadyObserved, and canonical continuation memory remains two-tiered: work-rag for current state, rag for durable lessons.
current.next_action: Continue consecutive autonomous implementation by rewriting to and executing the next exact documented slice in the same session after each commit; the current documented heuristic ladder starts at src/ez_ax/config/mcp_stdio.py and only permits FINAL_STOP after the documented queue, bounded resume-search, full heuristic-catalog sweep, and final exact gap re-evaluation are all honestly exhausted with no exact next one-commit slice below pageReadyObserved.

compression_policy.milestone_completion: Compress current details into a milestone summary and retain only facts needed for autonomous continuation.
compression_policy.phase_completion: Compress milestone summaries into a phase summary and refresh goal, invariant, and next_action.

history.milestone: Low-attention autonomous continuation remains unambiguous under the Python-first PRD contract
history.summary: Upgraded the low-attention operating contract from single-step closeout bias to consecutive autonomous looping: after each one-commit slice, work-rag must be rewritten to the next exact documented slice and the same session should continue unless scope, validation, or full exhaustion blocks it honestly.
